use std::collections::HashMap;
use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};
use tokio::sync::broadcast;
use tokio::time::{sleep, Duration};

enum CacheEntry {
    Ready(String),
    InFlight(broadcast::Receiver<String>),
}

struct Cache {
    store: Mutex<HashMap<String, CacheEntry>>,
}

async fn expensive_compute(key: &str) -> String {
    sleep(Duration::from_millis(100)).await;
    format!("computed_{}", key)
}

async fn get_or_compute(cache: Arc<Cache>, key: String) -> String {
    {
        let mut guard = cache.store.lock().unwrap();
        match guard.get(&key) {
            Some(CacheEntry::Ready(val)) => return val.clone(),
            Some(CacheEntry::InFlight(rx)) => {
                let mut rx = rx.resubscribe();
                drop(guard);
                // GOOD: wait on in-flight result instead of recomputing
                return rx.recv().await.unwrap();
            }
            None => {}
        }
        let (tx, _rx) = broadcast::channel(1);
        guard.insert(key.clone(), CacheEntry::InFlight(tx.subscribe()));
        drop(guard);

        // only this task computes
        let val = expensive_compute(&key).await;
        let mut guard = cache.store.lock().unwrap();
        guard.insert(key, CacheEntry::Ready(val.clone()));
        let _ = tx.send(val.clone());
        val
    }
}
